arts.ttl: Why Emi Martinez and Marco Asensio have not been included in Aston Villa's squad vs Brentford
arts.sum: Why Emi Martinez and Marco Asensio have not been included in Aston Villa's squad vs Brentford Emi Martinez and Marco Asensio are both absent from Aston Villa’s squad to face Brentford due to injury.

arts.ttl: Brentford vs Aston Villa LIVE match updates and team news from Gtech Community Stadium
arts.sum: They won seven of their opening eight league home matches this season but are now winless in the last six, drawing two and losing four. Villa have won just once in their last nine league fixtures on the road, having amassed 10 points from their opening four away fixtures this season.

arts.ttl: Leeds United linked with Aston Villa favourite Jack Grealish 'loved playing with'
arts.sum: Leeds United, well placed to seal a return to the Premier League from the Championship at the second attempt this season, have been linked with a move for former Aston Villa striker Tammy Abraham in the summer transfer window. Abraham, proven in the top two tiers of English football, spent the 2018-19 season at Villa, on loan at Chelsea, scored 26 goals in what proved to be a promotion winning season; the forward netted four goals in a single game, the 5-5 draw with Nottingham Forest, and one against West Brom in the play-off semi-finals.

arts.ttl: Manager drops big hint at future Aston Villa transfer – Clearly believes in player long-term
arts.sum: RB Leipzig manager Marco Rose has hinted that they intend to sign Kosta Nedeljkovic permanently from Aston Villa this summer. RB Live cover comments from the Leipzig manager made during his pre-match press conference this week, in which Nedeljkovic was a subject of discussion. The 19-year-old joined Leipzig on loan from Aston Villa in January The post Manager drops big hint at future Aston Villa transfer – Clearly believes in player long-term appeared first on Sport Witness.

Fixtures Stoke City U21 Vs. Aston Villa U21 - Sat, Mar 8th 2025, 12:00 Aston Villa were made to settle for a point on the road in Premier League 2 on Saturday afternoon, being held to a 1-1 draw by Stoke City at the bet365 Stadium. Josep Gombau’s Young Lions weathered an early Stoke storm in the Potteries to take the lead 17 minutes in through Kane Taylor. However, despite controlling large parts of the encounter, the Villans succumbed to a late Emre Tezgel equaliser from the penalty spot nine minutes from time, sending them home with just a point to show for their efforts. From the first whistle, Villa were on the attack, and could have taken the lead inside the opening minute, as Mason Cotcher pulled back for Jamaldeen Jimoh-Aloba, but he dragged his shot wide. Despite this, it was the hosts who began the affair the brighter of the two sides, enjoying much of the territory without creating anything clear cut in the first 10 minutes. And they would be made to rue not making their dominance pay, as Gombau’s side grew into the game and took the lead shortly after the quarter-hour mark. A neat, sweeping move saw the ball shifted from right to left. I-Lani Edwards found the onrushing, overlapping Taylor who fired into the far corner to put the Villans ahead. The visitors had the wind in their sails, and looked for more. Jimoh-Aloba drove forward and again looked for the far corner, flashing an effort this time more narrowly wide of the post. But the pendulum swung once more in the direction of the Potters, who finished the half the stronger, leaving Villa with Lander Emery to thank for keeping them in front. The visiting goalkeeper denied efforts from Sydney Agina and Tezgel in quick succession, before moments later rushing off his line to thwart Favour Fawunmi, who had been played in behind for Stoke’s best opportunity of the opening period. The second 45 followed a similar, end-to-end pattern to the first. It was all to play for in an open affair, with both sides looking to secure the points. Emery was again called into action within two minutes of the restart, beating away Bosun Lawal’s effort from distance. Stoke stopper Alfie Brooks then needed to be alert at the other end, doing well to deny Cotcher from close range after Bradley Burrowes had found the Villa forward on the edge of the six-yard box with a clever cut-back. Villa looked for a crucial second goal to kill the game, and it might have come 10 minutes from time when substitute Triston Rowe surged forward and fed Burrowes, but couldn’t quite manage to get a proper connection on the ball back into the box. Instead, it would be the home side who immediately raced down the other end to equalise, Tezgel slipping in behind and going down in the area under Thierry Katsukunya, who was booked for his troubles. Tezgel dusted himself down and made no mistake from 12 yards, sending Emery the wrong way to restore parity. Stoke had the bit between their teeth and piled on the pressure in the closing stages in search of an equaliser, with Fawunmi heading agonisingly wide at the far post, but the Villans successfully saw out the closing stages to secure a hard-earned point. arts.ttl: Aston Villa favoured over Newcastle United and 'vulnerable' Chelsea in Champions League race
arts.sum: Former Aston Villa striker Dwight Yorke believes that his old club can pip rivals including Chelsea, Newcastle United and Nottingham Forest to a place in the Champions League for next season, despite an inconsistent campaign up to now. Yorke, who made almost 300 appearances for Villa and scored shy of 100 goals, believes that while the ongoing Champions League campaign will make Villa's Premier League bid a little more difficult, they possess the manager and the squad to fight on both fronts and nip in ahead of their rivals come the end of the season.

arts.ttl: ‘Remain interested’ – Aston Villa still keen on player despite his injury, to make fresh approach in summer
arts.sum: Last summer, Sporting refused to let Pedro Gonçalves leave unless his €80m clause is activated after Aston Villa showed interest. The Villans wanted to sign the winger in the winter market as they looked to add to their squad. arts.ttl: £370m Aston Villa boost as Chris Heck speaks out
arts.sum: It has been reported that Chris Heck hopes Aston Villa's revenue will reach up to £370m for this season Those earnings includes gate receipts and income from UEFA's newly created value pillar on top of the £42m which the club will be awarded in prize money.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola sends Jack Grealish transfer message as Aston Villa return stance clear
arts.sum: Manchester City boss Pep Guardiola has insisted that he will only judge former Aston Villa star Jack Grealish for his performances on the pitch. Latest Aston Villa news from BirminghamLive as Man City boss Pep Guardiola opens up on former Villans star Jack Grealish, who has struggled for the Premier League champions recently

arts.ttl: Aston Villa mega store update as Bullring location revealed
arts.sum: Aston Villa's new mega store in Birmingham's Bullring shopping centre is taking shape ahead of its opening in May. Paul O'Brien, director of leasing and commercialisation at Hammerson, said: "Aston Villa’s new store coming to Bullring is a great example of Hammerson’s occupier mix strategy, delivering best-in-class spaces that are exciting and unique in equal measure.
